claim_tested: The repository owner reports that five developer text-versus-video comparisons were completed and that the video explanation was more reading-friendly overall.
limitations: No individual observation records, answers, timings, consent or eligibility confirmations, or protocol-deviation notes were supplied with the aggregate report.
More reading-friendly is a qualitative conclusion and is not equivalent to the predeclared video-win rule based on correctness and completion time.
This aggregate report cannot satisfy the observed-user-behavior threshold for H-LEARNING-001 until five anonymous raw observations are compiled by the registered experiment tool.
Participant-level contradictory or negative outcomes cannot be assessed from the aggregate conclusion.

measurements.metric: Aggregate qualitative conclusion favored video for reading friendliness

provenance.collection_method: Repository-owner statement in the active Codex conversation; no participant-level raw records were supplied.

sample_context.description: Repository owner reports five completed developer comparisons and an aggregate qualitative conclusion that video was more reading-friendly.
sample_context.population: Five developers reported by the repository owner; individual eligibility, repository familiarity, and task conditions are not independently verifiable
